diary.title: 🧃 A Juice Carton's Diary
diary.html:
Monday: Hello! I am a juice carton. Today I was born in a big factory. A machine made me from paper and plastic. Then a worker filled me with delicious orange juice.

Tuesday: A big truck took me to a stationery store... no wait, it was a supermarket! I am now on a shelf with many other cartons. We are all waiting for someone to take us home.

Wednesday: A girl picked me up! She put me in her bag. At lunchtime, she drank all my juice. Mmm, she liked it! Then she threw me in the blue recycling bin. Good girl!

Thursday: Today I went to a recycling plant. Big machines washed me and cut me into tiny pieces. They separated the paper from the plastic.

Friday: This is amazing! The factory used my paper to make a new notebook. I am not a carton anymore — I am a notebook now! A boy will write stories in me. The plastic part? It became part of a shirt! The recycling plant gave me a rescue. I have a new life!

Remember: when you recycle, old things become new things. Every carton, bottle and can can have a second life!

time.title: 🕐 Telling the Time
time.html:
Do you know how to tell the time in English? Let's learn!

When the long hand points to 12, we say o'clock. For example: 'It is three o'clock.' That means 3:00.

When the long hand points to 3, we say quarter past. For example: 'It is quarter past three.' That means 3:15. The long hand has moved a quarter of the way around the clock.

When the long hand points to 6, we say half past. For example: 'It is half past three.' That means 3:30. The long hand has moved half way around the clock.

When the long hand points to 9, we say quarter to. But be careful! Now we say the NEXT hour. For example: 'It is quarter to four.' That means 3:45. The long hand is almost at the top again, so we say the next hour — four!

Let's practise! 8:00 = eight o'clock. 8:15 = quarter past eight. 8:30 = half past eight. 8:45 = quarter to nine. Can you tell the time now?
